Sounding like distant thunder, the strange rumble from deep inside the building was unexpected as the floors and walls began to shake violently. Control panels inside Unit 4 of Chernobyl, or the Vladimir Ilyich Lenin Nuclear Power Plant, went berserk, flashing ominously as confused operators questioned what was happening during their safety test. The time was 1.24am, April 26, 1986. Moments later, the second explosion ripped through the reactor, spawning the worst nuclear accident in history. It unleashed 500-times the amount of radiation produced by the atomic bomb that flattened Hiroshima. Here, in scintillating detail, DEEP DIVE investigates how a botched safety drill was to blame.
  • 27 comments
  • share How the Chernobyl disaster unfolded, minute by minute

Like Meghan - if your relationships with everyone else are toxic, maybe you're the toxic one

She has been at the centre of one of Hollywood's most acrimonious and toxic divorce battles. Featuring restraining orders, accusations of financial ruin and abuse along with bitter public social media outbursts, the breakdown in the once-happy marriage between Alice Evans (right) and Ioan Gruffudd reads like the script of a long-running soap opera. While the dispute rumbles on in the US courts, Alice has revealed how she has endured further torment - through the loss of two of the most important figures in her life. Following the death of her brother Tony, aged just 55, last August, Alice has been left reeling after her father Professor David Evans (left), who was a distinguished academic and mathematician, passed away in March. This week the Vampire Diaries actress claimed how, in a further blow, she was unable to say a final farewell to her father after insisting she had not been invited to his funeral. Now the Daily Mail can reveal that a second extraordinary family feud led to Alice remaining behind in Los Angeles to grieve alone while her father was laid to rest more than 5,000 miles away.
  • 177 comments

But several branches around the UK have now been fitted with a so-called theft-proof counter, with all products stored behind it, out of reach of diners. The company's shop in West Croydon, south London, is among those which have been renovated and has now reopened its doors to customers in recent days. Best wishes to the family in carrying on this essential support for animals.

Speaking on behalf of the family, the couple's two daughters - Daisy and Emily - told how their mother's death at the age of 61 had been 'immensely shocking to all of us, and came with very little warning'. In a moving tribute to their 'extraordinary, kind and talented mother', the sisters, both acclaimed actresses, said of Sarah (left, with Anthony): 'We know how profoundly she has impacted the lives of so many, and we couldn't be more proud of the legacy she is leaving behind.' They were, in many ways, referring to the work of their mother - who the Daily Mail revealed this month had died from a rare and aggressive form of thyroid cancer - as an animal welfare campaigner. While supporting her husband on his journey from a TV ad heartthrob to international star, Sarah dedicated her life to running a sanctuary for rescued horses, ponies and donkeys and her work as a specialist dog behaviourist. Now the Daily Mail can reveal for the first time the extraordinary backstory of the heartbreaking double tragedy that set Sarah on her chosen path. Anthony, 72, and Sarah set up their 90-acre sanctuary in memory of their late 'dear friend' Cynthia Lund and her daughter Erin (right and inset) who tragically died aged just three after losing her own agonising fight with cancer.

This could happen to your daughter, brother, niece or even you! Have some empathy. Remember the person she was and leave her in peace. It is q tragedy. Don’t makre it worse for her family. I hope she gets medicated so she doesn’t hurt herself or others. I wouldn’t wish this fate on anyone.

The younger generations experience of the world is very different to the older ones. They have experienced the 2008 crash, suppressed wages and opportunities, austerity, closure of their youth facilities, massive student debt and now unaffordable homes and rents with the possibility of WW3 on the horizon. Even if they can afford to bring a child into this world why would they want to make another youngster suffer all the above?

From childhood, South Korean pop star Soyou dreamed about getting married, having three children and living in a house filled with the aroma of cooking. But like so many of her fellow female citizens, the 34-year-old singer - who became one of her nation's biggest stars with heartfelt songs about love, relationships and romance - has now abandoned such traditional ideas. In a candid interview last month, she disclosed that she decided during Covid that she did not want to bring a child into the world and had ruled out marriage. 'If I don't want children, why marry?' she said. Many other South Korean women are asking the same question - with the result that this highly successful Asian country is confronting the world's worst fertility crisis and arguably the most ferocious gender wars among younger generations.

There is a less invasive solution to this problem.I developed this intimate condition a few months after having my HRT coil removed. The whole nature of the skin in that area changed and the itching was unbearable. I was prescribed steroid and then hormone creams or ointments which didn't help or made things worse. Then a hospital consultant asked me to try a new prototype ointment, to be applied daily, and within a week, the itching and skin condition had completely recovered and returned to normal. Unfortunately, this was about 8 years ago and I can't remember the name of the ointment but it worked like a miracle.

  • I understand what David Haye means when he voices his 'controversial' views, but he just expresses them in such a desperately blunt and insensitive way that they will always attract criticism. He's bang on about the notion that Adam or anyone in there had a hard time. They were being paid over ten grand every day they were in there. That's a luxury, not having a tough time. With this sort of behavior he fits the profile of a Real Madrid player perfectly.

Fans have poked fun at Jude Bellingham after the Real Madrid star was caught play acting during his side's disastrous defeat by Real Betis.  The 22-year-old Englishman was caught by cameras appearing to fake being struck in the face by Sofyan Amrabat. The pair were battling for possession and the Moroccan was emerging with the ball when a stray arm went towards Bellingham.
